jokes.text: You're like a... a porridge, a cream of wheat, a farina, that kind of shit. That's what white people's skin tone is. It's just different kinds of hot cereal.
jokes.setup: Discussion about being happy with your skin color
jokes.punchline: White skin tones compared to bland breakfast cereals
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational comedy with perfect character voice. Leon's specific way of describing race through food metaphors is both unexpected and character-specific.

jokes.text: Black people, your skin tone's like, it depends on the amount of milk you put in the coffee. Fuckin' right. I'm like a fuckin' Yoo-hoo.
jokes.setup: Continuing the skin tone discussion
jokes.punchline: Self-identifying as chocolate milk drink
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that escalates the food metaphor. Yoo-hoo reference is specific and funny.

jokes.text: It's a little late, frankly, for the 'Happy New Years', you know? The statute of limitations has kind of run out on the 'New Years.' Three days. Plenty.
jokes.setup: Randi wishes Larry Happy New Year
jokes.punchline: Larry's arbitrary rules about when holiday greetings expire
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry pedantry about social conventions. Very character-specific nitpicking.

jokes.text: Larry's response to 'Happy New Year' - 'Eh' followed by complaint about timing
jokes.setup: Normal New Year greeting from Randi
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive 'Eh' and pedantic complaint about timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David character moment - finding fault with social conventions most people accept without thinking.

jokes.text: By the way, everything doesn't have to be happy. Why does everything have to be happy?
jokes.setup: Continuing complaint about 'Happy New Year'
jokes.punchline: Larry's contrarian philosophy about forced positivity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's perpetual contrarianism and negativity.

jokes.text: That's, that's huge. If you need some name tips, I'm your guy.
jokes.setup: Learning Randi is 8 months pregnant
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately offering unsolicited naming advice
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry presumption that his opinion is wanted. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Maybe something, uh, Swedish, perhaps? Yeah, I'm... my husband is African-American, so I'm not sure that would, I don't know, gel.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests Swedish names
jokes.punchline: Randi reveals her husband is African-American
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where Larry's suggestion becomes awkward. Well-structured setup and payoff.

jokes.text: You like saying that, don't you. Do I like saying that he's African-American? Yeah. I think you wanted me to know.
jokes.setup: Larry notices how Randi said 'African-American'
jokes.punchline: Larry accusing her of wanting to broadcast it
jokes.explanation: Larry's social paranoia showing. He reads ulterior motives into normal conversation.

jokes.text: You, you're jostling the fetus. You can't... You can't run in your condition.
jokes.setup: Larry sees pregnant Randi exercising
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt concern about 'jostling the fetus'
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriateness. The word 'jostling' is perfectly clinical and awkward.

jokes.text: Larry tells pregnant woman 'You're jostling the fetus' while she exercises
jokes.setup: Pregnant Randi working out at the gym
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate concern about 'jostling the fetus'
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry interference in others' business with medical advice he's unqualified to give.

jokes.text: Maybe you want to consult your blacksmith. See what he has to say about it.
jokes.setup: Randi mentions her 'midwife'
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically suggests consulting a 'blacksmith'
jokes.explanation: Sharp wordplay that mocks the antiquated sound of 'midwife'. Economical and biting.

jokes.text: I pity that fetus. Oh, my God. I can't watch this.
jokes.setup: Randi insists she can exercise while pregnant
jokes.punchline: Larry dramatically declaring he 'pities the fetus'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Larry's outrage. 'I pity that fetus' is quotably absurd.

jokes.text: You're over-talcing here. I couldn't even breathe in there.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about someone using too much talcum powder
jokes.punchline: Larry's specific complaint about 'over-talcing'
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry nitpicking about minor social infractions. 'Over-talcing' is a good made-up complaint.

jokes.text: My Auntie Rey put some of that shit on her, and went into anaphylactic shock. Yeah. Started shakin' and shit. Took her to the ER. Man, they had to pump her stomach for nothing.
jokes.setup: Discussion about talcum powder being bad for you
jokes.punchline: Leon's vivid story about his aunt's allergic reaction
jokes.explanation: Leon's storytelling voice with unnecessary medical details. Character-appropriate rambling.

jokes.text: Leon's story about his aunt's talcum powder anaphylactic shock
jokes.setup: Discussion about talcum powder dangers
jokes.punchline: Aunt going into shock and having stomach pumped 'for nothing'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the talcum powder danger for later payoff, reasonably funny on its own.

jokes.text: Know what I noticed? No one ever wears those hats on backwards. If mean, if you're gonna get your ass kicked, you want to see it coming.
jokes.setup: Seeing someone in a MAGA hat
jokes.punchline: MAGA hats never worn backwards because you need to see attackers coming
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational comedy with perfect logic. Original insight with great setup/punchline structure. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Yeah. Scones are supposed to be hard. This is like a muffin. Well, not really. It's supposed to be fresh. Yeah, fresh hard.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about a soft scone
jokes.punchline: Larry's insistence on 'fresh hard' as a contradiction
jokes.explanation: Larry's food pedantry beginning. His certainty about scone texture is character-perfect.

jokes.text: I'm not quite sure you know what a scone is, Mocha Joe. Oh, I know what a scone is. Do you? You may have a looser definition of scone than I do.
jokes.setup: Disagreement about scone quality
jokes.punchline: Larry's condescending expertise about scone definitions
jokes.explanation: Larry's pompous authority on trivial matters. Sets up the escalating coffee shop conflict.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'll keep the scone-slash-muffin. So you're gonna keep the scone? Yeah. Muffin. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Larry's compromise on the pastry dispute
jokes.punchline: Larry's petty insistence on calling it a 'muffin' after saying he'll keep it
jokes.explanation: Larry can't let it go even when accepting the item. Perfect petty character moment.

jokes.text: You're fuckin' up. Next.
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe says he doesn't have Danishes
jokes.punchline: Leon's blunt assessment that he's 'fucking up'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and Leon's direct style. Dismissive and funny.

jokes.text: Put your foot on it, like everybody else does. I'm gonna sit with my foot on the table? Yeah, that's how you hold it down. The floors are wobbly.
jokes.setup: Larry complains about wobbly table
jokes.punchline: Mocha Joe's absurd solution and blaming wobbly floors
jokes.explanation: Mocha Joe's defensive incompetence. Blaming floors instead of fixing the table is absurd.

jokes.text: Nobody likes a wobbly table. Fastest way to lose customers is wobbly tables. I got an uncle with a wobbly leg. I can't stand that motherfucker. Leaning on shit all the time.
jokes.setup: Larry giving business advice about wobbly tables
jokes.punchline: Leon's parallel about his uncle with a wobbly leg
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parallel between furniture and family members. Leon's specific complaint is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: This is cold coffee. Whoa, whoa. Wait a second. That coffee is not cold. Look, we don't wanna be fuckin' haters right now, but this shit is cold.
jokes.setup: Larry and Leon discover their coffee is cold
jokes.punchline: Leon's diplomatic 'we don't wanna be haters' followed by blunt honesty
jokes.explanation: Leon's attempt at diplomacy immediately undercut by harsh reality. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Would you stick your nose in a hot cup of coffee? Why would you stick your nose... Because I want to prove to you that it's cold coffee. Watch this. That's cold coffee.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to prove the coffee is cold
jokes.punchline: Larry putting his nose in the coffee as proof
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that fits Larry's obsessive need to prove points. Visual gag works well.

jokes.text: That doesn't prove anything except... it does. ...that you're an old, bald nut! What'd you say to me? You heard me. Now get out, you old, bald fuck!
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe dismissing Larry's coffee test
jokes.punchline: Escalation to personal insults and ejection
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to open conflict. Sets up Larry's vendetta against Mocha Joe.

jokes.text: Mocha Joe calling Larry 'an old, bald nut' and 'old, bald fuck'
jokes.setup: Argument escalating over coffee temperature
jokes.punchline: Mocha Joe's personal attacks on Larry's appearance
jokes.explanation: Standard insult humor that serves the escalation but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hey. Larry might be one or two of those things, but not all three.
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe calls Larry 'old, bald fuck'
jokes.punchline: Leon's diplomatic defense admitting Larry is partially described
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic defense that's both loyal and honest. Leon's diplomatic style with brutal honesty underneath.

jokes.text: He's eating out of my bowl. Oh, I just got that bowl from the kitchen. I thought it was an office bowl. It's an office bowl for humans. Not for dogs.
jokes.setup: Larry complains about dog eating from his bowl
jokes.punchline: Larry's specification that it's for humans, not dogs
jokes.explanation: Larry's germaphobia and need to specify the obvious. Character-appropriate pickiness.

jokes.text: It needs to be sterilized. I can't, I can't... share a bowl with a dog.
jokes.setup: Alice says she washes the bowl
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic need for sterilization
jokes.explanation: Larry's germaphobia escalating to medical terminology. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: All right, what... What the hell is that? Why is one eye closed? Oh, well, it's kind of personal. I don't share it with everybody. Yeah, it's very personal. The whole world can see it, but it's personal.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about Alice's visible tattoo
jokes.punchline: Larry pointing out the contradiction of public but 'personal' tattoo
jokes.explanation: Good logical contradiction Larry would definitely notice. Sharp observation about public privacy.

jokes.text: If I walked in here with a horn on my head, would you say, 'What are you doing with a horn on your head?' I'd say, 'Oh, I can't tell you. It's personal.'
jokes.setup: Larry frustrated by Alice's 'personal' tattoo excuse
jokes.punchline: Absurd horn on head analogy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd analogy that perfectly captures Larry's frustration. Visual absurdity works well.

jokes.text: Really? Then why didn't you put it on your ass?
jokes.setup: Alice says the tattoo is 'just for me'
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical suggestion about private placement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic cutting through pretentious explanations. Economical and sharp.

jokes.text: You can't clean your glasses with a shirt that I'm wearing while I'm wearing it. It's inappropriate. It's crossing a boundary.
jokes.setup: Alice uses Larry's shirt to clean her glasses
jokes.punchline: Larry's formal complaint about boundary crossing
jokes.explanation: Larry's personal space issues and formal language about minor infractions. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Jeff's immediate boycott declaration after learning about Larry being insulted
jokes.setup: Larry telling Jeff about being called old and bald
jokes.punchline: Jeff's instant, dramatic boycott decision
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's loyalty and tendency toward dramatic gestures.

jokes.text: Harvey Weinstein mistaken identity incident
jokes.setup: Jeff being mistaken for Harvey Weinstein at party
jokes.punchline: Woman's horrified reaction and accusation
jokes.explanation: Topical cringe moment but relies heavily on current events reference.

jokes.text: He looks like Harvey Weinstein. He looks just like him. What the hell? Okay. All the time, women, men... doesn't matter.
jokes.setup: Continuing the mistaken identity situation
jokes.punchline: Jeff's resigned acceptance that this happens regularly
jokes.explanation: Jeff's weary acceptance of being mistaken for a notorious figure. Character development through humor.

jokes.text: I've been following her around all night, but she never has any. They go fast. I don't care how foo-foo the other items are, and people love 'em, pigs in a blanket.
jokes.setup: Larry wanting pigs in a blanket at the party
jokes.punchline: Universal truth about pigs in a blanket popularity vs fancy food
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation about party food hierarchy. Larry's food obsessions showing.

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, the 16th president of these United States... Yeah, yeah. You're funny. ...Abraham Lincoln. Let's give him a big round of applause.
jokes.setup: Larry in a tall hat that resembles Lincoln
jokes.punchline: Susie's sarcastic presidential introduction
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Larry's hat choice. Susie's mocking tone fits their dynamic.

jokes.text: This hat is very similar to the one that Kate Middleton wore. Okay? You don't know shit. I know you look ridiculous. How about that?
jokes.setup: Susie defending her fashion choice
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt assessment despite his own appearance
jokes.explanation: Larry and Susie's typical adversarial dynamic. Both being ridiculous while criticizing each other.

jokes.text: Hey, you know what? Somebody has to stand up for that fetus. You don't know anything about fetuses. I know you don't go on a treadmill eight months pregnant.
jokes.setup: Susie criticizing Larry for harassing Randi
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-appointed role as fetal advocate
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier fetus concerns. Larry's righteousness about others' business is peak character.

jokes.text: Larry, Happy New Year. You're too late. 'Happy New Year.' Come on. It's three weeks. Isn't it almost a month already? It's ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Another person wishing Larry Happy New Year
jokes.punchline: Larry's continued enforcement of his greeting statute of limitations
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Larry's consistency. His arbitrary time limits are character-perfect.

jokes.text: How do you know it's a happy new year, anyway? I can't believe it. What? Aw, I missed 'em again. It's unbelievable.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning the assumption of happiness
jokes.punchline: Larry missing pigs in a blanket again while philosophizing
jokes.explanation: Larry's philosophical complaints interrupted by food obsession. Good character priorities.

jokes.text: He just talks about his shows all the time. His own show, and how about... Romano is on 500 times a day. Somebody Feed Phil. Oh, geez, with all the chaos in the world, in third-world countries, feed him? He should be feeding other people. It should be called Phil Feeds Everybody.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about Phil Rosenthal's shows
jokes.punchline: Larry's alternative show title about global responsibility
jokes.explanation: Larry's typical criticism of others' success with moral superiority angle. Good specific complaint.

jokes.text: You avoid the person all night... Of course. And then at the end, when you're about to leave, you go, 'Ah!' You give 'em a big goodbye. Then they feel good... Bye! Goodbye and good luck to you and your family. They're very happy that you spent this time with them at the end of the night, and... and you slip out. It's genius.
jokes.setup: Explaining The Big Goodbye strategy
jokes.punchline: The elaborate psychological manipulation described matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social strategy that reveals Larry's manipulative side. The detailed explanation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I like the other one. Well, I think the house is very nice. Did they throw that fish smell in for free?
jokes.setup: Ted complimenting the house during ventriloquist act
jokes.punchline: Dummy Larry insulting the house's smell
jokes.explanation: Good ventriloquist bit allowing Larry to insult the house. Fish smell is specific and insulting.

jokes.text: There's, there's Ted Danson. Wow, is he handsome! He sure thinks so.
jokes.setup: Dummy praising Ted's looks
jokes.punchline: Immediate undercut about Ted's vanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure. The compliment/insult combo is economical and sharp.

jokes.text: Susie, I love the curtains, but why did you make a jacket out of it?
jokes.setup: Complimenting the curtains
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Susie's outfit matches the decor
jokes.explanation: Classic insult disguised as observation. The curtain/clothing comparison is visually specific.

jokes.text: Are you having a good time? A good time? Are you kidding me? I'm with a bunch of stiffs, the food sucks, the drinks aren't strong enough, and your hand is up my ass.
jokes.setup: Larry asking dummy if he's having fun
jokes.punchline: Dummy's brutal honesty about the party and the ventriloquist situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor acknowledging the ventriloquist act while delivering harsh party criticism. Multi-layered comedy.

jokes.text: You don't need to call her when you land. If there's a plane crash, we'll know about it.
jokes.setup: Ted saying he'll call when he lands
jokes.punchline: Larry's dark logic about unnecessary check-in calls
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry dark logic about social conventions. Economical and brutally practical.

jokes.text: Sir, I need you to get out of here. Get out of here? What are you talking about? I need you to leave this room 'cause you've been ogling me... the entire night. Ogling you? Are you nuts? I, I, I've been ogling the pigs in a blanket.
jokes.setup: Valerie accusing Larry of inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Larry's innocent food obsession misinterpreted
jokes.explanation: Great callback turning Larry's food obsession into a misunderstanding. Perfect cringe comedy setup.

jokes.text: You giving me The Big Goodbye? The Big Goodbye? What are you... What are you talking about? Big Goodbye. Where you avoid me all night, and then you think you can cure it by having a Big Goodbye. I know. I've been around.
jokes.setup: Larry attempting The Big Goodbye on Phil
jokes.punchline: Phil recognizing and calling out the strategy
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback where Larry's strategy backfires. Phil's awareness makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Yeah. Everybody Loves Joam. It's fantastic. I get to go. And then I have the whole Somebody Feed Phil show. I want you to do my show. I want to go to Ethiopia with you.
jokes.setup: Phil describing his Portuguese Raymond adaptation
jokes.punchline: Portuguese version called 'Everybody Loves Joam'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective name adaptation joke. Shows Phil's international success obsession.

jokes.text: Did I ever tell you that I once did a, uh, premature Big Goodbye? So what does that mean? You said goodbye and then you didn't leave? I did a Big Goodbye and then I didn't... Come on. And I didn't leave.
jokes.setup: Larry describing a failed Big Goodbye
jokes.punchline: The awkwardness of saying goodbye then not leaving
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Big Goodbye concept. The social awkwardness is relatable and Larry-specific.

jokes.text: By the way, before we get into that, are you aware that you have a wobbly table here? Look at this. Look at this table. It's unbelievable. I, I don't know how you can go to bed at night.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment between Larry and Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately distracted by furniture problems
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Larry's obsessions override romance. Perfect character priority.

jokes.text: If all tables in your house wobbled, you could live like that? You could in a wobble-house?
jokes.setup: Cheryl says the table doesn't bother her
jokes.punchline: Larry's concept of living in a 'wobble-house'
jokes.explanation: 'Wobble-house' is a great coined term. Larry's escalation to absurd extremes is character-perfect.

jokes.text: I think when I'm with you, it makes me feel... ...better about myself? Morally superior. Yes. Yes. I hear that a lot. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Cheryl explaining why she's attracted to Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate understanding and frequent experience with this
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-aware character moment. Larry knows his role as moral comparison point. Very quotable.

jokes.text: But I feel that way with animals 'cause they're generally so stupid, and I can... especially insects, I can crush them. You know, and, and they eat each other. I mean, they're crazy, animals. So, yeah. I feel smarter and morally superior to them.
jokes.setup: Larry saying he feels morally inferior to people
jokes.punchline: Larry finding his moral superiority over animals and insects
jokes.explanation: Good character insight showing Larry's need for moral hierarchy. His animal logic is absurd but consistent.

jokes.text: If we ever got back together... Yeah. ...and somehow this table appeared on, on our bedside... Yeah? ...It would be fixed immediately. Immediately. Oh, my God.
jokes.setup: Larry hypothetically getting back together with Cheryl
jokes.punchline: His immediate condition about fixing the wobbly table
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry priorities - romance secondary to furniture stability. Great callback integration.

jokes.text: What? Where the fuck you been at? What are you? My mother? Fuck yeah, I am. All right, I was at a party, okay? I was at Jeff's party. That party was over a long time ago. Huh? Yeah. This right here... This right here? It's called tappin' hours.
jokes.setup: Leon questioning Larry's late return
jokes.punchline: Leon's concept of 'tapping hours' and maternal authority
jokes.explanation: 'Tapping hours' is a brilliant Leon coinage. His protective authority over Larry is funny role reversal.

jokes.text: This is the hours when people are tappin' ass. I'm not tappin'. I'm not tappin'. Older white men should not be out this fuckin' late. There's no late-night yacht club, or late-night garage sales and shit like that. You out here fuckin'.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining tapping hours
jokes.punchline: Leon's specific examples of what old white men should be doing late at night
jokes.explanation: Brilliant racial/age stereotyping with specific examples. 'Late-night yacht club' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Ah! You know what I'll do? I'll call you in the middle of lunch. Say it's an emergency and you gotta go. I did that the last time I had lunch with him. Oh, boy. That guy's relentless.
jokes.setup: Leon offering to help Larry escape lunch
jokes.punchline: Larry already used that strategy previously
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's repeated escape attempts and Phil's persistence. Good character development.

jokes.text: I'm not playing with him. He's a Trump supporter. Never play with a Trump supporter. Really? See him around town with that hat. 'Make America Great Again.' I don't need that crap. He just... He makes me want to not be anywhere near him.
jokes.setup: Discussing golf partner Arnie Norton
jokes.punchline: Larry's absolute policy against Trump supporters
jokes.explanation: Larry's political intolerance as character trait. Sets up the MAGA hat strategy later.

jokes.text: Wasn't that one of the shithole countries? I can't remember if that was on the list or not. Oh, look. I'm getting a thing. Oh, yeah. Oh, look at that. Um, uh... My son's flight, uh, got in early.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to get out of Ethiopia trip
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate Trump reference followed by fake emergency
jokes.explanation: Larry's inappropriate political reference combined with fake emergency escape. Character-appropriate social awkwardness.

jokes.text: Leon's road rage incident and near-violence
jokes.setup: Someone almost hitting Leon's car
jokes.punchline: Leon's explosive reaction and threats to drag driver from car
jokes.explanation: Standard road rage comedy that shows Leon's temper but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hey! What the fuck are you doing? Whoa. Oh, God. Oh, God. Motherfucker! What the fuck? What are you trying to do, man? What the fuck are you doing? You little fucker!
jokes.setup: Car accident or near-miss
jokes.punchline: Leon's explosive road rage reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard road rage scene. Leon's intensity is character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I told you, for the rest of my dying days, I will never come to this place with you. Ever. That's not what you said. I said I was... You said you're boycotting this place. And you're never coming back here. You didn't say with me. What are you? My Jewish puppet master?
jokes.setup: Jeff defending his boycott interpretation
jokes.punchline: Jeff's 'Jewish puppet master' accusation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Jeff's exasperated insult. The puppet master image is vivid and funny.

jokes.text: This coffee, by the way, is unbelievable. It's fantastic. Is it hot? It's very hot. Let me see that for a second. Hey, don't touch my... I don't like... Don't touch it. What the... Hey, stop it! What are you, a fuckin' goose?
jokes.setup: Larry testing Jeff's coffee temperature
jokes.punchline: Larry's nose-test repeated, Jeff's 'goose' insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Larry's coffee testing method. 'Fucking goose' is a creative insult for the behavior.

jokes.text: You know what, Larry? Get out. Huh. This is what it's come to. You're banned. For life. I never wanna see you in here again. Is that so? Yeah. You're banned. Banned! Get out!
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe reaching his limit with Larry
jokes.punchline: Official lifetime ban declaration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to official banishment. Sets up Larry's vengeful response.

jokes.text: Mark my words, Mocha Joe, and mark them well. I don't know how, and I don't know when, but I will exact vengeance. As God is my witness, I will bring you to the brink of extinction, or I will die trying.
jokes.setup: Larry being banned from Mocha Joe's
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic Scarlett O'Hara-style vengeance declaration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic escalation with Gone with the Wind reference. Perfect Larry overreaction to minor slight.

jokes.text: I was catering at a party. Larry was molesting me with his eyes the entire night. And then when I went into a pantry, he followed me in, cornered me, and he grabbed my breast.
jokes.setup: Valerie describing the alleged incident
jokes.punchline: Completely false version of Larry's innocent pigs in a blanket pursuit
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing how innocent behavior can be misinterpreted. Effective cringe comedy.

jokes.text: You know he did something like that to me, too? No. Oh, my God. Yeah. Everything was fine. And one day he... asked about a tattoo I have on my arm, and I wouldn't tell him what it meant, and then he got obsessive about it.
jokes.setup: Alice joining the accusations
jokes.punchline: Innocent tattoo questions reframed as harassment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how multiple innocent interactions can build a false pattern.

jokes.text: What's with the robe? Oh. 'Cause I spit cereal up all over my shirt, because there was dog hair in it. Disgusting. Right?
jokes.setup: Alice asking about Larry's appearance
jokes.punchline: Larry's innocent explanation about dog hair contamination
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier dog contamination concerns. Larry's germaphobia continues.

jokes.text: You're wearing a MAGA hat at a sushi restaurant? Is that true? Well, I did have the hat on, yeah. What, are you pro-Trump? No, no. Look, I got this idea to wear that hat so I wouldn't have to have lunch with Phil Rosenthal.
jokes.setup: Alice confronting Larry about the MAGA hat
jokes.punchline: Larry's explanation of the hat as social repellent strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback revealing Larry's strategy. Using political symbols as people repellent is genius.

jokes.text: You know, look. When you're a celebrity, you can do anything you want. You can grab 'em by the pussy... What did you say? What? Why are you wearing a bathrobe? And that hat!
jokes.setup: Larry quoting Trump while wearing the hat
jokes.punchline: Alice's horror at the combination of appearance and quote
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment with unfortunate timing of Trump quote and appearance. Good escalation.

jokes.text: What are you doin' here, Larry? I thought I told you to get out and not come back. Yeah, so you did, Mocha Joe. So you did. I've merely come to inform you that I have leased the place next door, where I'll be opening up a coffee shop exactly the same as yours. Only charging much lower prices. All for the express purpose of taking you down.
jokes.setup: Larry returning to Mocha Joe's after being banned
jokes.punchline: Larry's formal declaration of spite store war
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry escalation. The formal spite store declaration is both petty and grandiose.

jokes.text: You know what I'm gonna call it? What? Latte Larry's.
jokes.setup: Larry revealing his coffee shop name
jokes.punchline: Alliterative name that directly competes with Mocha Joe
jokes.explanation: Perfect petty naming choice. The alliteration and direct competition aspect is quintessential Larry.

jokes.text: I need that talcum powder. What you got goin' on? It's an emergency. Get me the talcum powder! Fuck! Who you tappin'? Cheryl. What? My man Larry David re-tappin' that ass!
jokes.setup: Larry frantically requesting talcum powder
jokes.punchline: Leon's realization about Larry's romantic situation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to talcum powder dangers. Leon's excitement about Larry's romantic life is character-perfect.

jokes.text: So sorry! Oh, my God. You look so much better. Do I? Oh. Are you kidding? I couldn't even look at you before. I mean, it was... really, like, hideous. You looked... disgusting. I could barely glance at you.
jokes.setup: Larry seeing Cheryl after her allergic reaction subsided
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutally honest assessment of how bad she looked
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriate honesty. His relief makes him say exactly the wrong thing.

jokes.text: You're such an asshole. You are such... I mean, I'm, I'm fighting for my life, and this is what you're... Susie? What are you guys doing here?
jokes.setup: Cheryl's outrage at Larry's insensitivity
jokes.punchline: Susie arriving at the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Susie's perfectly timed entrance. Sets up the revelation scene.

jokes.text: I knew it. I knew something was going on with you two. I could smell it. That night at our house. The ventriloquist. He drove you home. Something's been going on here. I knew it. I knew it! Bravo! Bravo, Poirot.
jokes.setup: Susie putting together the clues
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic congratulation comparing Susie to detective Poirot
jokes.explanation: Good callback integrating previous clues. 'Bravo, Poirot' is a perfect sarcastic response.

jokes.text: Because he just flew in from San Francisco and he texted me when he landed. I... Because he's her significant other, and you're her significant nothing! Just keep your fuckin' trap shut! Go!
jokes.setup: Explaining Ted's knowledge
jokes.punchline: Susie's brutal assessment of Larry as 'significant nothing'
jokes.explanation: 'Significant nothing' is a brilliant insult. Perfectly captures Larry's actual relationship status.

jokes.text: Oh, please. After what that fetus has been through, this'll be a day at the beach. Happy New Year!
jokes.setup: Randi blaming Larry for fetal endangerment
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive assessment based on her previous activities plus inappropriate greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback integrating multiple running gags. Larry's logic and inappropriate timing are quintessential.

jokes.text: Larry's shorts criticism: 'better to do it with pants on... It's hard to take a person seriously wearing shorts'
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe confronting Larry about spite store
jokes.punchline: Larry critiquing Mocha Joe's choice to wear shorts for confrontation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry observation about inappropriate clothing choices for serious situations.
